channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [RELTD] Ravindra Energy Board Meeting on April 29, 2026, to Approve Financial Results
item.description:
Ravindra Energy Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on April 29, 2026, to consider and approve the standalone and consolidated financial results and financial statements for the year ended March 31, 2026. The trading window for dealing in the company's securities has been closed for designated persons and their immediate relatives from April 1, 2026, and will remain closed until 48 hours after the declaration of the financial results.

  • Ravindra Energy board meeting set for April 29, 2026.
  • Meeting to approve FY26 standalone and consolidated financial results.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, until 48 hours post results.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ravindra Energy Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [HERANBA] Heranba Board to consider converting ICDs to OFCDs on April 27, 2026
item.description:
Heranba Industries Ltd. has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting for April 27, 2026. The main agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve a proposal for the conversion of Inter-Corporate Deposits (ICDs) previously granted by the company to its wholly-owned subsidiary, Heranba Organics Private Limited (HOPL), into Optionally Fully Convertible Debentures (OFCDs). The specific terms and conditions for this conversion will be finalized and approved during the board meeting. Additionally, the company noted that its trading window has been closed since April 1, 2026, and will reopen 48 hours after the declaration of the financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, in adherence to SEBI's insider trading regulations.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 27, 2026.
  • To consider converting ICDs to OFCDs for subsidiary HOPL.
  • Conversion terms and conditions to be approved by the board.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Heranba Industries Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [PUSHPIN] Pushpsons Resubmits Q4 FY19 Results, Correcting Missing Pages
item.description:
Pushpsons Industries Ltd has re-submitted its audited financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2019. The re-submission addresses an accidental omission of two pages, the Un-Qualified Declaration (Form-A) and the Independent Auditor's Report, from the original filing on May 30, 2019. The company confirmed that the financial figures in the re-submitted results are identical to those previously filed, ensuring full compliance after receiving directions from the authorities. The filing includes the complete financial statement for the period.

  • Pushpsons re-submitted Q4 FY19 audited financial results.
  • Original May 30, 2019 filing accidentally missed two pages.
  • Missing pages were Un-Qualified Declaration and Auditor's Report.
  • Financial figures in the re-submitted results are unchanged.
  • Re-submission ensures full regulatory compliance.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PUSHPSONS INDUSTRIES LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [MUTHOOTFIN] Muthoot Finance Declares 30 Interim Dividend; TDS Rules Apply
item.description:
Muthoot Finance Ltd. announced an interim dividend of 30 per equity share for the financial year 2025-26, declared on April 10, 2026. The record date for determining eligible shareholders is April 17, 2026. Following the new Income-tax Act, 2025, effective April 1, 2026, the company will deduct tax at source (TDS) on this dividend. The TDS rate will depend on the shareholder's residential status and the documents submitted. For resident individual members, no TDS will be deducted if the aggregate dividend for the tax year does not exceed 10,000. Otherwise, a 10% TDS applies for those with a valid PAN, and 20% for those without. Exemptions are available for shareholders submitting Form 121 or specific orders/certificates, and for certain entities like AIFs, Provident Funds, and some Mutual Funds/Insurance Companies upon submission of required declarations.

  • Muthoot Finance declared 30 interim dividend for FY26.
  • New Income-tax Act, 2025, mandates TDS on dividends.
  • TDS rates vary by shareholder residency and document submission.
  • Resident individuals may get no TDS if dividend is below 10,000.
  • Shareholders must update PAN and submit forms for exemptions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: MUTHOOT FINANCE LTD. | Category: Dividend

item.title: [ASALCBR] AABL gets NCLT nod to acquire SDF Industries for 30.85 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Associated Alcohols & Breweries Limited (AABL) has received approval from the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) to acquire SDF Industries Ltd. for 30.85 crore. SDF Industries, a distillery-cum-bottling unit in Thrissur, Keralam, will become a wholly-owned subsidiary of AABL upon completion of the acquisition. The facility boasts an IMFL bottling capacity of approximately 3.60 lakh cases per annum and an ENA distillery license capacity of 75 lakh liters per annum. This strategic acquisition aims to bring AABL's bottling operations in Keralam in-house, enhancing operational control, efficiency, and supporting future growth plans. AABL plans to upgrade the facility, with operations targeted to commence by September 2026, expecting improved margins and new product launches.

  • AABL secured NCLT approval to acquire SDF Industries Ltd. for 30.85 crore.
  • The acquisition includes a distillery-cum-bottling unit in Keralam.
  • It adds ~3.60 lakh cases/annum IMFL bottling and 75 lakh liters/annum ENA capacity.
  • This move aims to bring bottling in-house, improving efficiency and control.
  • AABL expects enhanced margins and new product launches post-acquisition.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ASSOCIATED ALCOHOLS & BREWERIES LTD. | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [PRECISION] East India Drums Bags ₹2.99 Cr Order from ITBP Guwahati
item.description:
🔥 Trending

East India Drums and Barrels Manufacturing Ltd has secured a Letter of Acceptance from the Indo Tibetan Border Police (ITBP) – Guwahati. The order is for the supply of Steel Drums/Barrels, valued at ₹2,98,77,397. These barrels will be supplied to Guwahati. This order represents a significant milestone, reinforcing the company's ongoing relationship with ITBP and highlighting its capability to provide high-quality industrial packaging solutions for India's energy and defense sectors.

  • Secured order from ITBP for steel drums/barrels.
  • Order valued at ₹2.99 crore.
  • Strengthens company's presence in defense sector.
  • Reaffirms capability in industrial packaging solutions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PRECISION CONTAINEURS LTD. | Category: Deals

item.title: [ICODEX] Icodex to Avail ₹10.8 Cr Credit Facilities from ICICI Bank
item.description:
Icodex Publishing Solutions Ltd's Board of Directors approved availing credit facilities from ICICI Bank, Viman Nagar Branch, aggregating to ₹10.8 crore. The funds will be used for working capital, general corporate purposes, and capex through a Credit Arrangement Letter (CAL). The borrowings are within the limits of Section 180(1)(c) of the Companies Act, 2013, and the creation of charge on the company's assets will comply with Section 180(1)(a) of the Act.

  • Icodex board approves availing ₹10.8 Cr credit from ICICI Bank.
  • Funds earmarked for working capital, general corporate needs, and capex.
  • Borrowings are within the limits of Companies Act, 2013.
  • Charge creation on assets will comply with relevant regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Icodex Publishing Solutions Ltd | Category: Loan & Debt

item.title: [SPMLINFRA] SPML Infra allots 42.45 lakh equity shares at ₹215/share
item.description:
SPML Infra Ltd's Board of Directors approved the allotment of 4,244,844 equity shares, each with a face value of ₹2, at a price of ₹215 per share (including a premium of ₹213). This preferential allotment was made to both Promoter Group and non-promoters who exercised their rights attached to an equal number of warrants. The allotment was conducted in accordance with SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018.

  • SPML Infra allotted 4,244,844 equity shares.
  • Shares were allotted at ₹215 per equity share, including a ₹213 premium.
  • Allotment was on a preferential basis to Promoter Group and non-promoters.
  • This followed the exercise of rights attached to 4,244,844 warrants.
  • The total value of the allotment is approximately ₹91.26 crore.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SPML Infra Limited | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [CAPRO] Caprolactam Chemicals: Reg 23(9) of SEBI (LODR) Not Applicable
item.description:
Caprolactam Chemicals Ltd is not required to comply with Regulation 23(9) of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, concerning Related Party Transaction disclosures. As of March 31, 2025, the company's paid-up equity share capital is ₹4,60,00,000 and its net worth is ₹4,95,89,569, falling below the threshold of ₹10 crore paid-up equity share capital and ₹25 crore net worth as specified in Regulation 15(2) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015.

  • Caprolactam Chemicals exempt from Related Party Transaction disclosure under Reg 23(9).
  • Paid-up equity share capital is ₹4.6 Cr.
  • Net worth is ₹4.96 Cr as of March 31, 2025.
  • Exemption due to falling under the threshold specified in Regulation 15(2).

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CAPROLACTAM CHEMICALS LTD. | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [ABBOTINDIA] Abbott India: Board Changes & Postal Ballot Approved
item.description:
Abbott India Ltd's board meeting on April 22, 2026, addressed key management changes. Ms. Anisha Motwani's term as Independent Director ends on April 24, 2026. Mr. Neeraj Jain is appointed as an Additional Independent Director effective April 25, 2026, subject to member approval. Ms. Alison Davies resigned as Director effective April 22, 2026. Mr. James Wenner is appointed as Additional Non-Executive Director effective April 23, 2026, subject to member approval. Mr. Tushar Singh will transition to another Abbott affiliate on June 1, 2026, ceasing to be Senior Management Personnel on May 31, 2026. Mr. Amol Metkar is appointed as Plant Director - Goa and Senior Management Personnel effective June 1, 2026. The board also approved a postal ballot for the appointment of Mr. Jain and Mr. Wenner.

  • Anisha Motwani's term as Independent Director ends April 24, 2026.
  • Neeraj Jain appointed as Additional Independent Director, effective April 25, 2026, pending member approval.
  • Alison Davies resigned as Director, effective April 22, 2026.
  • James Wenner appointed as Additional Non-Executive Director, effective April 23, 2026, pending member approval.
  • Amol Metkar appointed as Plant Director - Goa and Senior Management Personnel, effective June 1, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ABBOTT INDIA LTD. | Category: Board Meeting
